evals.prompt: Do a competitor analysis for our project management SaaS. Main competitors: Linear, Jira, Asana, Monday.com. Focus on features, pricing, target audience, and where we can differentiate with our AI-powered task prioritization.
evals.expected_output: Structured analysis with feature matrix, pricing comparison, target segments, and differentiation strategy

assertions.name: feature_matrix
assertions.description: Feature comparison across all 4 competitors with specific capabilities

assertions.name: pricing_comparison
assertions.description: Pricing tiers documented with dates verified

assertions.name: target_audience
assertions.description: Target audience identified per competitor

assertions.name: differentiation
assertions.description: Recommendations for AI task prioritization as differentiator

assertions.name: sources_cited
assertions.description: Data sources noted for verification

evals.prompt: Compare authentication solutions for our SaaS: Auth0, Clerk, Supabase Auth, and building our own with Passport.js. Evaluate: pricing at 10K MAU, features, developer experience, lock-in risk, and time to implement.
evals.expected_output: Technical comparison with cost analysis, feature gap, implementation effort, and recommendation

assertions.name: pricing_at_scale
assertions.description: Cost comparison at 10K MAU with specific numbers

assertions.name: feature_comparison
assertions.description: Feature matrix (social login, MFA, SSO, webhooks)

assertions.name: implementation_effort
assertions.description: Estimated time to implement each option

assertions.name: lock_in_risk
assertions.description: Vendor lock-in assessment and migration difficulty

assertions.name: recommendation
assertions.description: Clear recommendation with rationale

evals.prompt: Research the competitive landscape for our AI-powered code review tool. Who are the main players? What do they charge? Where are the gaps?
evals.expected_output: Market landscape with key players, pricing, feature gaps, and opportunity analysis

assertions.name: key_players
assertions.description: 3-5 competitors identified with brief profiles

assertions.name: feature_analysis
assertions.description: What each tool does well and where it falls short

assertions.name: pricing_documented
assertions.description: Pricing tiers with date verified

assertions.name: market_gaps
assertions.description: Underserved needs or missing features identified

assertions.name: not_marketing_copy
assertions.description: Analysis based on capabilities, not marketing claims

evals.prompt: We're choosing between Vercel, Railway, and Render for deploying our Next.js + NestJS app. Compare: pricing (we expect 50K requests/day), deployment experience, scaling, database support, and cold start times.
evals.expected_output: Platform comparison with cost modeling, deployment workflow, and scaling characteristics

assertions.name: cost_modeling
assertions.description: Monthly cost estimate at 50K requests/day for each platform

assertions.name: deployment_comparison
assertions.description: Deployment workflow compared (git push, CLI, Docker)

assertions.name: scaling_comparison
assertions.description: Auto-scaling capabilities and limitations

assertions.name: database_support
assertions.description: Managed database options and pricing

assertions.name: recommendation
assertions.description: Recommendation based on the specific use case

evals.prompt: Analyze 3 open-source alternatives to our commercial analytics product: Plausible, Umami, and PostHog. Compare: self-hosting effort, feature parity, community health, and total cost of ownership.
evals.expected_output: Open-source comparison with self-hosting assessment, feature gaps, and TCO analysis

assertions.name: feature_comparison
assertions.description: Feature matrix comparing each to our product

assertions.name: self_hosting_effort
assertions.description: Infrastructure requirements and maintenance overhead

assertions.name: community_health
assertions.description: GitHub stars, contributors, release frequency, issue response time

assertions.name: tco_analysis
assertions.description: Total cost including hosting, maintenance, and opportunity cost

assertions.name: honest_assessment
assertions.description: Acknowledges where open-source options are better, not just worse
